title: elaphoglossum viscidulum
intro: Elaphoglossum viscidulum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the sticky elaphoglossum or sticky creeping fern. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum viscidulum include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent infections and promote wound healing. Elaphoglossum viscidulum can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
